heading: Protection of certain files of the Office of the Director of National Intelligence
label: 3146 Protection of certain files of the Office of the Director of National Intelligence
notes: References in TextThe Federal Rules of Civil Procedure, referred to in subsec. (f)(2)(D), are set out in the Appendix to Title 28, Judiciary and Judicial Procedure.
CodificationSection was formerly classified to prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.section 432d of this title

children.heading: Inapplicability of FOIA to exempted operational files provided to ODNI
children.label: a Inapplicability of FOIA to exempted operational files provided to ODNI

children.text: Subject to paragraph (2), the provisions of that require search, review, publication, or disclosure of a record shall not apply to a record provided to the Office of the Director of National Intelligence by an element of the intelligence community from the exempted operational files of such element.section 552 of title 5

children.label: A
children.text: contains information derived or disseminated from an exempted operational file, unless such record is created by the Office for the sole purpose of organizing such exempted operational file for use by the Office;

children.label: B
children.text: is disseminated by the Office to a person other than an officer, employee, or contractor of the Office; or

children.label: C
children.text: is no longer designated as an exempted operational file in accordance with this subchapter.

children.heading: Effect of providing files to ODNI
children.label: b Effect of providing files to ODNI
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this subchapter, an exempted operational file that is provided to the Office by an element of the intelligence community shall not be subject to the provisions of that require search, review, publication, or disclosure of a record solely because such element provides such exempted operational file to the Office.section 552 of title 5

children.heading: Search and review for certain purposes
children.label: c Search and review for certain purposes

children.text: United States citizens or aliens lawfully admitted for permanent residence who have requested information on themselves pursuant to the provisions of section 552 or 552a of title 5.
Any special activity the existence of which is not exempt from disclosure under the provisions of .section 552 of title 5

children.label: A
children.text: The Select Committee on Intelligence of the Senate.

children.label: B
children.text: The Permanent Select Committee on Intelligence of the House of Representatives.

children.label: C
children.text: The Intelligence Oversight Board.

children.label: D
children.text: The Department of Justice.

children.label: E
children.text: The Office of the Director of National Intelligence.

children.label: F
children.text: The Office of the Inspector General of the Intelligence Community.

children.heading: Decennial review of exempted operational files
children.label: d Decennial review of exempted operational files

children.text: Not less than once every 10 years, the Director of National Intelligence shall review the exemptions in force under subsection (a) to determine whether such exemptions may be removed from any category of exempted files or any portion thereof.

The review required by paragraph (1) shall include consideration of the historical value or other public interest in the subject matter of the particular category of files or portions thereof and the potential for declassifying a significant part of the information contained therein.

children.label: A
children.text: Whether the Director has conducted the review required by paragraph (1) before the expiration of the 10-year period beginning on , or before the expiration of the 10-year period beginning on the date of the most recent review.October 7, 2010

children.label: B
children.text: Whether the Director of National Intelligence, in fact, considered the criteria set forth in paragraph (2) in conducting the required review.

children.heading: Supersedure of other laws
children.label: e Supersedure of other laws
children.text: The provisions of this section may not be superseded except by a provision of law that is enacted after , and that specifically cites and repeals or modifies such provisions.October 7, 2010

children.heading: Allegation; improper withholding of records; judicial review
children.label: f Allegation; improper withholding of records; judicial review

children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), whenever any person who has requested agency records under alleges that the Office has withheld records improperly because of failure to comply with any provision of this section, judicial review shall be available under the terms set forth in .section 552 of title 5section 552(a)(4)(B) of title 5

children.label: A
children.text: In any case in which information specifically authorized under criteria established by an Executive order to be kept secret in the interests of national defense or foreign relations is filed with, or produced for, the court by the Office, such information shall be examined ex parte, in camera by the court.

children.label: B
children.text: The court shall determine, to the fullest extent practicable, the issues of fact based on sworn written submissions of the parties.

children.label: i
children.text: When a complainant alleges that requested records were improperly withheld because of improper exemption of operational files, the Office may meet the burden of the Office under by demonstrating to the court by sworn written submission that exempted files likely to contain responsive records are records provided to the Office by an element of the intelligence community from the exempted operational files of such element.section 552(a)(4)(B) of title 5

children.label: ii
children.text: The court may not order the Office to review the content of any exempted file in order to make the demonstration required under clause (i), unless the complainant disputes the Office’s showing with a sworn written submission based on personal knowledge or otherwise admissible evidence.

children.label: D
children.text: In proceedings under subparagraph (C), a party may not obtain discovery pursuant to rules 26 through 36 of the Federal Rules of Civil Procedure, except that requests for admissions may be made pursuant to rules 26 and 36 of the Federal Rules of Civil Procedure.

children.label: E
children.text: If the court finds under this subsection that the Office has improperly withheld requested records because of failure to comply with any provision of this section, the court shall order the Office to search and review each appropriate exempted file for the requested records and make such records, or portions thereof, available in accordance with the provisions of (commonly referred to as the Freedom of Information Act), and such order shall be the exclusive remedy for failure to comply with this section.section 552 of title 5

children.label: F
children.text: If at any time following the filing of a complaint pursuant to this paragraph the Office agrees to search each appropriate exempted file for the requested records, the court shall dismiss the claim based upon such complaint.

The term “exempted operational file” means a file of an element of the intelligence community that, in accordance with this subchapter, is exempted from the provisions of that require search, review, publication, or disclosure of such file.section 552 of title 5
Except as otherwise specifically provided, the term “Office” means the Office of the Director of National Intelligence.
